subject: [PATCH] mm: switch deferred split shrinker to list_lru

reviews.author: Johannes Weiner (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged that the swapoff path needs to drop the per-vswap spinlock before calling try_to_unmap(), agreed to restructure in v2.
reviews.raw_body:
Fixing David's email address. Sorry! Full quote below.

On Wed, Mar 11, 2026 at 11:43:58AM -0400, Johannes Weiner wrote:

reviews.author: David (Arm)
reviews.summary: The reviewer, David, did not provide any technical feedback or comments related to the patch, instead mentioning that they still receive emails sent to an old address due to a mailing list subscription.
reviews.raw_body:
I still catch everything sent to the old address as long as a mailing
list I am subscribed to is CCed :)

-- 
Cheers,

David

2026-03-11.patch_summary: This patch updates the deferred split shrinker to use list_lru, which properly sets up per-node and per-cgroup lists. This change improves handling of cgroups on NUMA systems by avoiding splitting large pages across nodes. The patch also streamlines list operations and reclaim walks, and adds calls to create node heads for cgroups when splittable pages are created. Additionally, it introduces helpers to the list_lru API for caller-side locking. The deferred split queue lock is replaced with a more efficient approach using list_lru's locking mechanisms.
